summary: Session 1 of 3: established docs/concepts/tier-model.md as the single source of truth for the Full vs Lightweight tier model (router-off, not Python-off; tier: is the single switch; both tiers share the Python lifecycle), grounded in ai_router/runtime_mode.py. Authored the docs/templates/consumer-bootstrap/ bundle (bundle README contract + spec.md.template + schemaVersion-4 session-state.json.template + start-here.md.template with the verbatim machine-checkable active-set rule + engine-file shared body + claude/agents/gemini tails). Documented tier + verificationMode in docs/spec-md-schema.md and fixed its stale 'runs without ai_router' framing. Reconciled adoption-bootstrap.md and quick-start.md to the D3 uniform scaffold (both tiers scaffold .venv + dabbler-ai-router + three engine files + start-here.md + templated spec; only divergence is Full-only router config). Pointerized the README tier highlight and the Get Started wizard copy (both-tier Python prerequisite, accurate Lightweight framing, SSoT pointer). No extension code changed and no release, per the spec. Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4) reached VERIFIED after a 3-round fix loop; 1 Critical was an empirically-confirmed false positive (spec config blocks carry no schemaVersion field).

reason.specifics: Session 2 implements the TS shared template writer + fixes gitScaffold/sessionGenPrompt/wizard to render the Session-1 template bundle; same orchestrator keeps full architectural context of the SSoT, the placeholder-token contract, and the TOTAL_SESSIONS repetition rule just authored.
